title: Ranunculus oinonenii
intro: Ranunculus oinonenii, also known as Oinonen's buttercup, is a species of flowering plant in the buttercup family (Ranunculaceae) native to Finland. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of various health conditions, including arthritis, gout, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Ranunculus oinonenii include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, the plant is often prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture to take advantage of its health benefits, although its use should be approached with caution due to potential toxicity.
taxonomy: Ranunculus oinonenii is a species of flowering plant in the family Ranunculaceae. It belongs to the genus Ranunculus and the order Ranunculales. The plant's classification is also reflected in its placement within the clade of flowering plants, specifically in the group known as eudicots. The family Ranunculaceae, to which it belongs, consists of about 2,000 species, many of which are herbaceous perennials. As a member of the Ranunculaceae family, Ranunculus oinonenii shares characteristics with other plants in this group.
